JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL WAS SIGNED BY JUDGE MAHONEY AND FILED FEBRUARY 13, 2009. THE COURT LOSES JURISDICTION TO RULE ON A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WHEN JUDGMENT IS ENTERED. SEE APRI INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 176, 181. PRIOR RECONSIDERATION MOTION WAS DENIED, AND NO NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW ARE RAISED BY CURRENT RECONSIDERATION MOTION. LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS CHALLENGING THE DENIAL OF EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT BENEFITS. THE BENEFITS WERE DENIED IN CASE #A0-138442 BY DEFENDANT ON OCTOBER 27, 2006 AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT TAKE A WRIT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1094.5, FROM THAT DECISION. THE DECISION BY JUDGE BERGER IN CASE #1942825 ONLY ALLOWED PLAINTIFF TO REOPEN THAT CASE, NOT THE PRIOR CASE (#1892564/A0-138442). PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SOUGHT A WRIT FROM ANY ACTION TAKEN BY DEFENDANT IN CASE #1942825. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD FACTORS DEMONSTRATING THAT HE CAN MAINTAIN A CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT OR THAT ANY SUCH CLAIM WOULD NOT BE BARRED BY THE 6 MONTH STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLICABLE TO CCP SECTION 1094.5. SEE SINETOS V. DMV (1984) 160 CAL. APP. 3D 1172, 1176. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Motion For Reconsideration (1) To Re-Open (2) For Equitable Relief After Judgment Denial Of Due Process Failure To Provide An Answer
Off Calendar Nov-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB Motion For Reconsideration (1) To Re-Open (2) For Equitable Relief After Judgment Denial Of Due Process Failure To Provide An Answer. Appearances: Plaintiff in Pro Per Arthur Webb appearing and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Motion does not comply with code of civil procedure section 1008(b). Judgment of dismissal was signed by Judge Mahoney and filed February 13, 2009. The Court loses jurisdiction to rule on a motion for reconsideration when judgment is entered. See APRI Inc. Co. vs. Superior Court (1999) 76 Cal.App. 4th 176, 181. Prior reconsideration motion was denied and no new or different facts, circumstances, or law are raised by this reconsideration motion. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charlton Holland of the Attorney General's Office (703.5719) for defendant and moving party
Arthur Webb (no phone number given) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff is challenging the denial of Employment Development Department benefits. The benefits were denied in case #A0-138442 by defendant on October 27, 2006 and plaintiff did not take a writ pursuant to CCP section 1094.5, from that decision. The decision by Judge Berger in case #1942825 only allowed plaintiff to reopen that case, not the prior case (#1892564/A0-138442). Plaintiff has not sought a writ from any action taken by defendant in case #1942825. Plaintiff has not plead factors demonstrating that he can maintain a claim against defendant or that any such claim would not be barred by the 6 month statute of limitations applicable to CCP section 1094.5. See Sinetos v. DMV (1984) 160 Cal. App. 3d 1172, 1176. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Plaintiff is granted leave to amend. The amended complaint to be filed by September 8, 2008. Mr. Webb to give notice of the ruling. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
